subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV/EFF, World Bank, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Argentine official (INDEC, BCRA) + Argentine/Spanish-language press.

POSITIVE-SKEW, high-variance reform-turnaround case: scored the Milei trajectory (fiscal surplus, disinflation, capital-control lifting) against a deep crisis baseline - genuine asymmetric upside with a real crisis tail.

INDEC credibility was restored after the 2007-2015 manipulation era. Source-lean: economic sources skew pro-Milei/market; press-freedom/rights sources skew critical - roughly bias-cancelling; Spanish-language press under-read.

economic.notes: A dramatic reform turnaround (fiscal surplus, collapsing inflation, lifted capital controls) on a fragile track record and resource upside.

fiscal_state.notes: A first primary surplus in ~two decades (~1.3% GDP) ended chronic deficits; durability rests on un-passed pension/tax reforms and political will. (IMF EFF 2025; OECD)

monetary_independence.notes: Genuine disinflation, but the BCRA's independence is historically weak and the 2026 crawling-band framework is criticized as fragile. (BCRA; PIIE)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The peso is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Dramatic: from ~211% YoY (Dec-2023) to ~31% (2025); still high in absolute terms, expectations not yet anchored. (INDEC; BCRA)

banking_stability.notes: Small (private credit ~10% GDP), dollarization-prone but de-dollarizing, well-capitalised; thin and shock-sensitive. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Vaca Muerta shale (record output), agri-commodities, and world #3 lithium exports - real export upside as trade opens; commodity concentration the caveat. (S&P Global; US ITA)

institutional.notes: Restored statistical credibility and a leaner state, against a historically weak judiciary and press-freedom backsliding.

rule_of_law.notes: Historically weak/politicized; heavy decree (DNU) use raises separation-of-powers concerns; improving-but-questionable. (WJP 2025; V-Dem)

statistical_integrity.notes: INDEC credibility RESTORED post-2015; data now IMF-cited and trusted. (INDEC; IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Slashed under austerity (mass dismissals, ministries cut); near-term capacity loss, possible long-term leaner efficiency. (OECD)

press_freedom.notes: RSF fell ~47 places under Milei (named a 2025 'Predator'); dismantled public media, hostility to press. (RSF 2025; PEN)

political_social.notes: Volatile reform politics and a poverty spike now easing, with an open, welcoming residency regime.

political_stability.notes: An Oct-2025 midterm landslide secured a veto/decree-blocking third but no majority - dependent on fragile blocs; recurring austerity strikes. (Americas Quarterly)

civil_liberties.notes: A functioning democracy with free elections and intact rights; protest-policing and press hostility are drags. (V-Dem; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Open, lawful, durable (rentista/ancestry routes, Spanish-speaking, citizenship after just 2y, dual allowed); a 2025 decree cut temp-resident access to public services. (DNM)

social_cohesion.notes: Poverty spiked then fell sharply (to ~28% by H2 2025); high polarization and economic stress persist but the trajectory is easing. (INDEC; UCA)

demographics.notes: A relatively favorable age structure and moderate fertility; brain-drain emigration the watch-item. (UN)

geopolitical.notes: A pro-US tilt and no external conflict, with capital controls largely lifted.

alliance_reliability.notes: A strong pro-US/Israel tilt (a US swap line, a shift away from BRICS/China), pragmatic; durability tied to Milei's tenure. (CFR)

conflict_involvement.notes: No external conflict; only a dormant Falklands/Malvinas claim. (background)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: The 'cepo' capital controls were largely LIFTED in 2025 (free FX, a floating band); not sanctioned, with reversal risk if reserves stress. (MercoPress; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Decent healthcare and moderate crime, against decaying infrastructure and drought exposure.

climate.notes: Recurrent drought hits agriculture/FX hard; otherwise temperate with a low catastrophe tail. (NASA-class)

healthcare.notes: Mixed public/private, decent access and outcomes; austerity strains public provision. (PAHO)

infrastructure.notes: Decaying and chronically underinvested; Vaca Muerta pipelines a bright spot. (OECD; S&P)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate (national homicide ~4.3/100k); Rosario narco-violence elevated but down to a 17-yr low, durability questioned. (InSight Crime)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low insurance penetration; low seismic/hurricane exposure limits the tail. (hazard context)

summary: A high-variance, positive-skew reform-turnaround: a first fiscal surplus in two decades, inflation collapsing from ~211% toward ~31%, lifted capital controls, restored statistical credibility, an open and welcoming residency regime, and real resource upside (Vaca Muerta shale, lithium, agriculture). The crisis tail is real - a fragile monetary framework and weak track record, a historically weak judiciary, press-freedom backsliding, decaying infrastructure, and reform politics dependent on fragile coalitions.
